nodes.label: Motion Effect Glossary
nodes.rationale: Curated snapshot mirroring the project's /vocabulary page; descriptions are authoritative and must be quoted verbatim so users always get consistent naming for motion effects.

nodes.label: Review Animations Skill
nodes.rationale: Default to flagging; approval is earned. A transition that runs but feels sluggish, lands from the wrong origin, or drops frames is a regression, not a pass.

nodes.label: Ten Non-Negotiable Animation Standards
nodes.rationale: Every animation in a diff is measured against justified motion, frequency-appropriateness, responsive easing, sub-300ms budgets, origin correctness, interruptibility, GPU-only properties, accessibility, asymmetric timing, and cohesion; a violation is a finding.

nodes.label: Aggressive Escalation Triggers
nodes.rationale: Flag-on-sight patterns (transition: all, scale(0), ease-in on UI, layout-property animation, ungated hover) that are always findings regardless of context.

nodes.label: Remedial Preference Hierarchy
nodes.rationale: Prefer earlier moves over later ones: delete the animation before reducing it, fix easing before origin, make it interruptible before polishing — removing motion is safer than tuning it.

nodes.label: Animation Frequency Table
nodes.rationale: The more often an action is seen, the less it should animate; keyboard-initiated actions never animate because they repeat hundreds of times daily and animation makes them feel slow and disconnected.

nodes.label: Easing Decision Rules
nodes.rationale: ease-out for enter/exit because it starts fast and feels responsive; ease-in is banned on UI because it delays the exact moment the user is watching. Built-in CSS easings are too weak — use strong custom cubic-beziers.

nodes.label: Per-Element Duration Budgets
nodes.rationale: UI animations stay under 300ms: button press 100-160ms, tooltips 125-200ms, dropdowns 150-250ms, modals/drawers 200-500ms; faster motion makes the interface feel faster at the same actual speed.

nodes.label: Spring Animation Configuration
nodes.rationale: Springs settle on physics parameters rather than fixed duration and maintain velocity when interrupted (keyframes restart from zero), so they suit gestures users may reverse mid-motion; keep bounce subtle (0.1-0.3).

nodes.label: Interruptibility (Transitions vs Keyframes)
nodes.rationale: CSS transitions can be retargeted mid-animation while keyframes restart from zero, so anything triggered rapidly (toasts, toggles) must use transitions or springs; @starting-style enables JS-free entry.

nodes.label: GPU-Only Property Rule
nodes.rationale: Only transform and opacity skip layout/paint and run on the GPU; Framer Motion x/y/scale shorthands run on the main thread via rAF and drop frames under load — use the full transform string; never drive child transforms via a parent CSS variable (style recalc storm).

nodes.label: Reduced Motion Accessibility
nodes.rationale: Reduced motion means fewer and gentler animations, not zero — keep opacity/color transitions that aid comprehension, drop movement; gate hover motion behind (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine) because touch fires false hovers on tap.

nodes.label: Graphify Build Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Two-track extraction: deterministic AST for code (free, no API key ever required) plus LLM semantic extraction only for docs/papers/images; a code-only corpus skips semantic extraction entirely, and the host agent itself acts as the LLM when no Gemini key is set.

nodes.label: Parallel Semantic Extraction Subagents
nodes.rationale: All subagents must be dispatched in a single message to run in parallel; subagent_type must be general-purpose because Explore is read-only and silently drops chunk files written to disk.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Cache
nodes.rationale: Cached per-file extraction results are checked before dispatching subagents so only uncached files cost tokens; stale .graphify_cached.json from a prior run is deleted so Part C never merges it (#1392).

nodes.label: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: Read-only diagnostic run before labeling that surfaces edge collapse, dangling/missing endpoints, and self-loops — the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches; it warns but never aborts.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge (use AMBIGUOUS when unsure), always show token cost, show raw cohesion scores, warn before visualizing more than 5000 nodes — the audit trail is the product.

nodes.label: Shrink Guard (#479)
nodes.rationale: to_json refuses to write when the new graph is smaller than the existing graph.json, and the empty-graph guard checks immediately after build, so an empty or partial extraction never clobbers a good graph, report, or analysis sidecar (#1392).

nodes.label: Fast Path: Query Existing Graph
nodes.source_location: ## What You Must Do When Invoked
nodes.rationale: If graphify-out/graph.json exists and the request is a natural-language question, skip the entire build pipeline and run graphify query immediately — the graph is already built.

nodes.label: Graphify Add URL and Watch Mode
Graphify MCP Server
nodes.rationale: Stdio MCP server exposing query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, shortest_path; Claude Desktop config must use the absolute interpreter path because it cannot run shell substitution.

nodes.label: Node ID Format (Full-Path Stem)
nodes.rationale: IDs use the full repo-relative path stem (every directory level) so same-named files in different directories stay distinct and semantic IDs match the AST extractor's; the older immediate-parent format created orphan ghost-duplicate nodes, and chunk suffixes are banned because IDs must be deterministic from the label alone.

nodes.label: Discrete Confidence Rubric
nodes.rationale: EXTRACTED is always 1.0; INFERRED must pick one of 0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55 — the discrete rubric replaced a continuous range because production showed a bimodal collapse with over half of edges defaulting to 0.5.

nodes.label: GitHub Clone and Cross-Repo Merge
nodes.rationale: The skill pipeline writes all outputs to graphify-out/ in the cwd, so monorepo subfolders must use the CLI directly (which nests graphify-out inside each scanned path) and then merge-graphs at the root to avoid clobbering.

nodes.label: Commit Hook and CLAUDE.md Integration
nodes.rationale: Post-commit hook re-runs AST extraction on changed code files only (no LLM, no background process); graphify claude install writes a CLAUDE.md section so the graph is checked before answering codebase questions in every session.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query CLI matches nodes by case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language matching, so the question must be expanded to up to 12 tokens drawn only from the graph's actual vocabulary — inventing tokens is forbidden, and an empty match means saying so rather than fabricating a search.

nodes.label: Work Memory Feedback Loop (save-result / reflect)
nodes.rationale: Every answered query is saved back with save-result and an --outcome (useful/dead_end/corrected) so graphify reflect can compile LESSONS.md; future sessions start from preferred sources, skip known dead ends, and see prior corrections — a self-improving loop.

nodes.label: Whisper Domain-Hint Prompt
nodes.rationale: The host LLM composes a one-sentence domain hint from god-node labels and exports it as GRAPHIFY_WHISPER_PROMPT, improving transcription accuracy without a separate API call; JSON output is written from Python because Whisper progress on stdout would corrupt a shell redirect (#1392).

nodes.label: Incremental Update and Cluster-Only
nodes.rationale: Code-only changes skip semantic extraction entirely (AST only, no LLM); cluster-only is self-contained and must not re-run Steps 5-9 because their intermediate files were deleted by the prior build's cleanup (#1392).

nodes.label: build_merge Replace-on-Re-extract
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction is preserved (#801); prune_sources is only for genuinely deleted files because changed files are reconciled by dropping every re-extracted source_file from the base before merge (#1344), and root= relativizes paths so pruning and manifests stay portable (#1361, #1417).

nodes.label: PhraseLoop App
nodes.rationale: Next 16 + Turbopack dev server at localhost:3000/app; only one dev server per checkout because a second corrupts .next/dev/cache; UI is PT-BR for sub-B1 profiles.

nodes.label: PhraseLoop API Surfaces
nodes.rationale: /api/discover (SSE YouTube import), /api/discover/clip (per-phrase WAV slice), /api/tts, /api/transcribe, /api/pronunciation/assess, /api/status; the 409 model_not_ready responses themselves kick off model downloads.

nodes.label: Playwright GUI Drive
nodes.rationale: Uses the cached Chromium via playwright-core with executablePath and locale pt-BR; fresh browser context means fresh IndexedDB and fresh onboarding; window.confirm is auto-dismissed so destructive Settings actions are safe in scripts.

nodes.label: PHRASELOOP_DATA_DIR Isolation
nodes.rationale: The real macOS data dir (~/Library/Application Support/PhraseLoop) is shared with the packaged Electron app, so verification must always point PHRASELOOP_DATA_DIR at a temp dir to avoid corrupting real user data; it also simulates a fresh install with no models.

nodes.label: Codex spawn_agent Dispatch
nodes.rationale: Codex lacks the Agent tool, so parallel extraction uses spawn_agent/wait_agent/close_agent (requires multi_agent = true in ~/.codex/config.toml) and collects results in memory — there are no per-chunk files on disk, so invalid JSON, not a missing file, is the failure signal.

nodes.label: Graphify Add URL and Watch Mode (Codex)
Extraction Subagent Prompt Spec (Compact, Codex)
nodes.rationale: Compact variant of the extraction prompt: same rules, node-ID format, and confidence rubric, but the subagent returns JSON inline instead of writing a CHUNK_PATH file, matching Codex's in-memory collection.

Distilled from Emil Kowalski's design engineering philosophy

confidence_score is REQUIRED on every edge
Step 1 - Write the Whisper prompt yourself
This must match the ID the AST extractor generates
